reviews.review_description:
It's really simple and easy to splice a clip or two together. You're limited to 30 second exports for the free version and you can upgrade for unlimited exports. I'll be using this to edit and share clips for sure!

Update as of 5/13/2026: New feature that EASILY let's you zoom in on a segment of a long video. You can further zoom in until you get to a manageable segment. Refine the clip you want and boom! Super fast clip!  I love it!

I like to save short Walkabout's Mini Golf clips and send them to friends. This is an easy way to edit out all the setup and stitch multiple clips together in one. This isn't going to replace a full pc editor, but it's a great extra tool to have and super easy to use. Highly recommend if you have a casual need for a light editor in headset.

reviews.review_title: Free Easy To Use VR Clip Editor!
reviews.review_description: ClipKit is actually super nice for quick edits on Quest. It’s really simple to use, you just pick clips, trim them, and export fast without dealing with complicated stuff. Perfect if you just wanna make clips quickly and get them posted. The background colours are beautiful also!

It's not free at all. 3.99 pay to export. What's the point. The editing barely works, no scrub, no real edit tools. The dev put more time in advertising his other apps. The other 5star review is definitely a paid review because nothing about this is 5star

developer_response.body:
The app is free to use, including trimming and previewing clips directly on the headset. The optional purchase simply unlocks exporting longer clips, which also supports my development of the app. Short exports are already available for free.

Scrubbing and timeline seeking are supported (including joystick controls), so if that didn’t work for you, it may be worth trying again or reaching out so I can fix the issue.

ClipKit is intentionally focused on quick trimming rather than full desktop-style editing tools. The goal is fast, simple clip creation directly in VR.

reviews.review_title: Easy to use. Free. Need I Say More?
reviews.review_description: Yes. ClipKit is a great clip editor right on your headset. No editing on whatever device you send it to, with whatever method you send it do. It's all right here. I will be clip farming my Dungeons of Eternity runs with this to be sure.

reviews.review_title: Very useful and free to use!
reviews.review_description:
The other review about it having to pay is wrong, it's free to clip and export clips up to 30 seconds, which is useful enough.

Very useful for quick trims and clipping all inside the headset

This is very neat for low-effort posts, but I would love a more in-depth editor, with all the bells and whistles.

Any chance you could get all the options and features from OpenShot Video Editor? I'd easily pay $20 for something like that. I'd pay 3x that for something as sophisticated as CapCut 🙏

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback! 🙏 ClipKit is designed to be simple and fast for VR, so I focused on the essentials to make quick edits easy.

I totally understand wanting more advanced features like OpenShot or CapCut. More tools are definitely something I can explore over time, but my goal for ClipKit right now is to keep editing lightweight, smooth, and easy to use inside the headset.

Really appreciate the suggestion, it helps me understand what people would value in future updates.
